name: Hard 10-Query Multi-Document Test Suite
description: Deliberately difficult queries requiring cross-document reasoning, multi-spec comparison, complex table extraction, and nuanced refusal. Designed to stress-test the full RAG pipeline.

qa_pairs.question: Compare the minimum yield strength of S32205 (UNS) across ASTM A789, A790, and A872. Which specification requires the highest yield strength and why might they differ?
What is the complete chemical composition range for chromium, molybdenum, and nitrogen in UNS S31803 per ASTM A790? Include both min and max percentages for each element.
For API 6A wellhead equipment rated at 15,000 psi working pressure, what material class requirements apply and what are the corresponding temperature ratings?
Compare the heat treatment requirements for duplex stainless steel between A789 tubing and A790 pipe. Are the solution annealing temperature ranges identical?
What is the minimum tensile strength for L80 grade casing per API 5CT, and how does this compare to the yield-to-tensile ratio requirements?
What is the maximum allowable carbon content for S31803 duplex stainless steel according to both A789 and A790? Are they consistent across both specifications?
What is the creep rupture strength of S32205 duplex at 600°C per ASTM A790?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: REFUSE - Creep rupture data is not included in ASTM A790. A790 covers mechanical properties at room temperature only. Elevated temperature properties would be found in ASME or specialized materials handbooks.

qa_pairs.question: According to API 5CT, what is the recommended torque for making up L80 casing connections in the field?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: REFUSE - API 5CT covers casing and tubing specifications but field makeup torque values are typically provided by connection manufacturers (e.g., VAM, Tenaris), not in the base 5CT specification.

qa_pairs.question: What product forms and manufacturing methods are covered by ASTM A1049, and what is the minimum yield strength for the F51 grade designation?
List all the PSL (Product Specification Level) categories defined in API 6A and explain what distinguishes PSL-1 from higher levels in terms of quality requirements.
